sense.definition: a type of cricket match that lasts up to five days between international teams

examples.x: He made his debut in Test cricket last summer.

sense.definition: a format of cricket where each team faces a fixed number of overs, usually 50, in a single day

examples.x: Australia won the One Day International series against India.

sense.definition: a classification of limited-overs cricket matches, typically 40 to 60 overs per side, played at a high domestic or international level
the highest standard of domestic cricket played over three or more days

examples.x: He made his debut in first-class cricket at the age of 19.
First-class cricket matches can last up to five days.
Scoring a century in first-class cricket is a significant achievement.
Many international players started their careers in first-class cricket.
First-class cricket is crucial for developing future Test players.

sense.definition: a format of the game where each team is allowed a fixed number of overs to bat, typically 20 or 50

examples.x: Limited overs cricket has become very popular for evening matches.
Limited overs cricket often finishes in a single day, unlike Test matches.
In limited overs cricket, each team bats for a maximum of 50 overs.
The World Cup is played in a limited overs cricket format.
Strategy in limited overs cricket focuses on scoring quickly.

sense.definition: a variant of cricket played in an enclosed indoor space, typically on a smaller pitch with modified rules

examples.x: We play indoor cricket when it's raining.
Tournaments attract indoor cricket players.
Schools offer indoor cricket in winter.
Facilities provide turf for indoor cricket.

sense.definition: a shorter, fast-paced, and exciting form of cricket where each team gets twenty sets of six throws to bat
(cricket) the scoring of four or six runs by hitting the ball to or over the designated outer edge of the field

examples.x: She hit a powerful shot over the bowler's head for a boundary.
His powerful shot sailed over the fielder's head, securing a boundary for his team.
The batsman's precise placement allowed him to notch another boundary.
The batsman's aggressive approach paid off with yet another boundary.
The bowler's frustration was evident as another boundary was conceded.

sense.definition: a batsman scoring 100 or more runs in a single innings

examples.x: Despite the loss, the highlight of the match was Smith's magnificent century.
The captain's century led the team to a comfortable victory.
The young prodigy stunned everyone by scoring a century in his debut match.
Virat Kohli hit his 50th century in international cricket.
It was a thrilling match as both openers managed to score a century each.

sense.definition: (cricket) a score of zero runs by a batsman in their innings

examples.x: He was disappointed to record a duck in the match.
The opener was bowled out for a duck in the first over.
His duck added pressure on the middle-order batsmen.
The team's score suffered after several batsmen were dismissed for ducks.
She survived the initial overs and was relieved not to be out for a duck.

sense.definition: a batsman scoring fifty runs in a single innings

examples.x: The opening batsman achieved a half-century with some elegant strokes.
She celebrated her half-century with a confident boundary shot.
The team's score improved significantly after his half-century contribution.
Scoring a half-century boosted his confidence for the rest of the match.
The young cricketer's first half-century was a memorable moment for his team.

sense.definition: the act of a batsman being declared out by the official who oversees the cricket match

examples.x: The dismissal of the batsman occurred when the ball hit the stumps.
His dismissal came after he edged the ball to the wicketkeeper.
The umpire raised his finger to signal the dismissal of the batsman.
The dismissal of the opener put pressure on the batting side.
The dismissal was controversial as the batsman claimed he didn't touch the ball.

sense.definition: a single delivery bowled by the bowler toward the batsman in cricket

examples.x: The bowler delivered a fast ball aimed at the stumps.
The bowler's slow ball deceived the batsman completely.
The bowler's first ball was a fast yorker.
The batsman confidently drove the ball through the covers for four runs.
The ball swung late, surprising the batsman.

sense.definition: (cricket) the contact between the bat and the ball resulting in the ball going toward the fielding team

examples.x: If there's an edge, it means the ball deflected off the bat to the fielding side.
He got out with an edge to the wicketkeeper.
An edge occurs when the ball touches the bat and goes to the opposing team.
A slight edge was enough for the catch.
The edge was caught by the fielder.

sense.definition: the batting turn of a team during which their players attempt to score runs against the bowling of the opposing team

examples.x: The team scored 300 runs in their first innings.
He played a crucial innings, scoring a century under pressure.
Her innings helped the team chase down the target with ease.
The bowlers struggled to take wickets in the opposing team's innings.
His quick-fire innings of 50 runs shifted the momentum in their favor.

sense.definition: (cricket) a batsman being dismissed when when the ball hits the stumps before they reach the crease while running

examples.x: The batsman was run out trying to steal a quick single.
The fielder's accurate throw led to a run out.
A brilliant piece of fielding resulted in a run out.
The umpire had no hesitation in raising his finger for the run out dismissal.
The team lost a crucial wicket to a run out in the final over.

sense.definition: a situation that happens when a team is made to bat again immediately after their first innings because they scored significantly fewer runs than the opposing team in their first innings

examples.x: Despite a good effort, the team was forced to follow-on due to a deficit of runs.
The bowlers struggled to take wickets after the follow-on was enforced.
The captain enforced the follow-on after their team gained a substantial lead.
The opposing team avoided the follow-on by batting defensively in their second innings.
He was disappointed after his team was forced to follow-on in the match.

sense.definition: (cricket) the act of delivering the ball to the batsman with the aim of dismissing them or preventing runs

examples.x: Good bowling is essential for restricting the opposing team's score.
The spinner took five wickets in a single innings with his sharp bowling.
The coach emphasized the importance of line and length in effective bowling.
Bowling is as much about strategy and consistency as it is about skill and speed.

sense.definition: a period of six balls bowled by a cricketer during which no runs are scored by the batsman

examples.x: She achieved her first maiden over in international cricket.
The bowler bowled a maiden over to increase the pressure on the batting side.
The spin bowler's maiden over was crucial in slowing down the run rate.
His consistent line and length helped him bowl a maiden over.
The opening bowler's maiden over set the tone for the team's bowling performance.

sense.definition: an illegal delivery bowled by a bowler, resulting in a penalty against the bowling team

examples.x: The bowler's delivery was too high, and the umpire signaled a no ball immediately.
The bowler's foot crossed the line, resulting in a no ball being called by the umpire.
The team conceded an extra run due to repeated no balls during the over.
The fast bowler struggled with his run-up, resulting in several no balls being bowled.
A no ball was called as the bowler's back foot was not behind the bowling crease.

sense.definition: a type of delivery that spins in the opposite direction to what is expected, often deceiving the batsman

examples.x: The bowler surprised the batsman with a well-disguised googly.

sense.definition: a delivery that reaches the batsman without bouncing on the pitch in cricket

examples.x: The bowler accidentally delivered a full toss, which was easily hit for a boundary.
A well-placed full toss can be a surprise weapon for a bowler.
He smashed the full toss straight over the bowler’s head.
The full toss was called a no-ball by the umpire.
She drove the full toss to the cover boundary with ease.

sense.definition: the role of the cricket player who stands behind the stumps to catch missed balls and attempt dismissals

examples.x: Good wicketkeeping can prevent many runs.
Efficient wicketkeeping is crucial for catching edges.

sense.definition: a batsman being dismissed when the wicketkeeper catches the ball after it touches the bat in cricket

examples.x: The batsman was dismissed with a brilliant caught behind by the wicketkeeper.
The fast bowler celebrated after getting a crucial caught behind early in the innings.
It was a close call, but the batsman was given out caught behind.
The bowler appealed confidently for a caught behind, and the umpire agreed.
The batsman couldn't believe it when he was given out caught behind.

sense.definition: a method of dismissal in cricket where the wicketkeeper removes the bails to dismiss a batsman out of their crease

examples.x: The wicketkeeper executed a perfect stumping to dismiss the batsman.
The crowd erupted in cheers as the wicketkeeper completed a sensational stumping.
The bowler deceived the batsman, leading to an easy stumping opportunity.
The batsman's foot slipped, leaving him vulnerable to a stumping.
The wicketkeeper's lightning reflexes made the stumping look effortless.

sense.definition: an extra run scored when the batsman misses the ball and it passes the wicketkeeper, allowing runners to run

examples.x: The team scored four byes when the ball went past the wicketkeeper.
He missed the ball, but they managed to run two byes.
A wild delivery resulted in several byes for the batting team.
The bowler's wide led to additional byes.
The keeper's mistake allowed three byes.

sense.definition: (cricket) a run scored when the ball hits the batsman's body, not the bat

examples.x: They scored a leg bye after the ball hit the batsman's pad.
The team gained a run through a leg bye.
The ball deflected off his thigh for a leg bye.
A leg bye was awarded when the ball hit her leg.
They added a leg bye to their total score.

sense.definition: (cricket) to play a shot by hitting the ball delivered by the bowler with the bat held horizontally and swinging it in a downward direction towards the leg side

examples.x: The batsman expertly hooked the short ball from the fast bowler over the square leg boundary for six.
He got into position quickly and hooked the ball fiercely, sending it racing towards the boundary.
The bowler tried to unsettle the batsman with a short delivery, but he was ready and hooked it powerfully for four runs.
The batsman's ability to hook the short ball effectively made him a dangerous player against fast bowlers.
With the fielders placed on the off side, the batsman saw an opportunity and hooked the ball over the leg side for four.

sense.definition: (cricket) a line marked on the pitch that defines the area within which a batsman must stand to be safe from being run out

examples.x: The batsman must reach the crease to avoid being run out.
He was safe because his bat was behind the crease.
She stood just inside the crease, ready to face the next ball.
A quick dive got him back into the crease just in time.
The bowler stepped over the crease, resulting in a no-ball.

sense.definition: (cricket) a batsman is dismissed when the ball hits their leg instead of the stumps

examples.x: She was trapped leg before wicket on the first ball.
He was dismissed leg before wicket by the fast bowler.
The player was out leg before wicket, missing a straight delivery.
The batsman reviewed the leg before wicket call.
The spinner got him out leg before wicket with a sharp turner.

sense.definition: a situation in cricket where a batsman is out when a fielder catches the ball on the full after the batsman hits it

examples.x: The fielder’s quick reflexes resulted in a stunning caught.
The team celebrated the caught that sealed their victory.

sense.definition: (cricket) a ball that bounces just in front of the batsman, making it challenging to play

examples.x: The bowler delivered a tricky half volley that the batsman struggled to defend.
He timed the half volley perfectly and drove it through the covers for four.
The batsman leaned forward to block the half volley with a straight bat.
It was a clever tactic to bowl a series of half volleys to induce a false shot.
The half volley rose sharply off the pitch, surprising the batsman.

sense.definition: a shot in cricket where the batsman switches hands and stance to hit the ball to the opposite side of their normal direction

examples.x: The batsman executed a perfect reverse sweep to beat the fielder at short third man.
She surprised everyone with a reverse sweep, catching the bowler off guard.
His reverse sweep went straight to the boundary for four runs.
The captain encouraged him to practice the reverse sweep to diversify his shots.
Despite the risky nature of the shot, he attempted the reverse sweep and succeeded.

sense.definition: a batting stroke played to hit the ball square on the leg side behind square, typically against a spin bowler

examples.x: The batsman executed a perfect sweep shot to dispatch the spinner to the boundary.
She attempted a sweep shot but missed, getting struck on the pads instead.
His sweep shot was mistimed and resulted in an easy catch for the fielder.
The batsman used the sweep shot effectively to counter the turning pitch.
A well-timed sweep shot can put pressure on the bowler in spin-friendly conditions.

sense.definition: (in cricket) a ball delivered by the bowler that is too far from the batsman to hit, usually penalized with an extra run to the batting side

examples.x: With nerves showing, the bowler delivered a wide that sailed over the batsman's head.

sense.definition: to dismiss a batsman by knocking over their stumps with the ball in cricket

examples.x: The bowler stumps the batsman with a quick delivery.
The wicketkeeper is stumping the batsman after a failed attempt at a sweep shot.
The umpire raised his finger quickly after the bowler stumped the batsman.
The bowler aimed perfectly and stumped the opener on the first ball.

sense.definition: a delivery in cricket bowled by a fast bowler aimed at the batsman's feet, typically difficult to play

examples.x: The yorker proved effective in the last match.
He unleashed a yorker that surprised everyone with its speed.
He has a lethal yorker in his bowling arsenal.
She practiced bowling yorkers relentlessly.
The yorker was instrumental in restricting the batting side's score.

sense.definition: (cricket) a statistic that shows how many runs a batsman scores on average per innings

examples.x: His batting average improved after scoring a century.
The player’s batting average is calculated by dividing total runs by the number of innings played.
A batting average above 50 is considered excellent in cricket.
His consistent performance has led to an impressive batting average.
She aims to finish the season with a batting average over 45.

sense.definition: (of cricket ball) to turn and move off the pitch after bouncing

examples.x: With sharp seam movement, the fast bowler's delivery cut sharply off the pitch.
The off-spinner's delivery cut sharply after bouncing, spinning past the outside edge.
